bids.reasoning: I am targeting maximum utility at the opening since there is no time pressure yet. The opponent’s first offer gives me low utility overall, with concessions only on issueD and issueE, so I will keep issueD as valueB since it is equally best for me while holding firm on the other high-value issues.

I am targeting maximum utility because it is still very early at t=0.02 and there is no deadline pressure to concede. The opponent’s second offer is worse for me than their opening offer, so they are not showing meaningful concession; with no messages to weigh, I will keep a strong bid while avoiding an exact repeat of my previous offer.

I am targeting maximum utility because it is still very early (t=0.04), so there is little pressure to concede on issues that matter to me. The opponent’s offers remain very low for my utility and mainly repeat valueC on high-weight issues, though they seem willing to give issueE:valueA and sometimes issueD:valueB; since issueD:valueB is equally good for me as valueA, I can offer that without losing utility.

At t=0.06 it is still very early, so I am targeting a near-maximal utility bid rather than conceding on important issues. The opponent’s valid offers give me low utility and do not show meaningful concession, alternating mainly on issueD while holding issueA/B/C/F at my worst values. I will avoid repeating my immediately previous bid by switching issueD back to valueA, which is still optimal for me.

I am targeting maximum utility because it is still very early (t=0.08) and there is no need to concede much yet. The opponent is holding firm on low-utility values for issueA, issueB, issueC, and issueF, while repeatedly offering issueE=valueA and sometimes issueD=valueB; since issueD=valueB is equally good for me as valueA, I can use that harmless flexibility.

I am targeting maximum utility (1.00) because it is still very early at t=0.10 and the opponent has not shown meaningful concession, repeatedly offering bids worth only about 0.28–0.44 to me. Their offers consistently concede only issueE while demanding low-value outcomes on my important issues, so I will hold firm and avoid repeating my exact previous bid by switching issueD to an equally valuable option for me.

I am targeting maximum utility (1.00) because it is still very early at t=0.12 and there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent has been holding firm on low-utility offers for me, alternating only issueD while keeping issueA/issueB/issueC/issueF at valueC; since their latest offer uses issueD:valueB and valueB is equally best for me on issueD, I can match that without conceding utility.

I am targeting maximal utility because it is still early (t=0.14) and the opponent has not shown meaningful concession, repeatedly offering very low values for my high-weight issues A, B, and C. Their only acceptable signal is sometimes using issueD:valueB, but issueD:valueA and valueB are equally good for me, so I can vary issueD while holding firm elsewhere and avoid repeating my exact previous bid.

I am targeting maximum utility (1.00) because it is still early at t=0.16 and there is no evidence the opponent is conceding on the high-importance issues. Their offers consistently give me very low values on issueA, issueB, issueC, and issueF, while only alternating on issueD; since issueD valueB is equally good for me as valueA, I can align with their recurring issueD preference without sacrificing utility.

I am still targeting a near-maximal utility bid because the negotiation is early at t=0.18 and the opponent has not shown meaningful concession; their offers remain low for me, around 0.28–0.44 utility depending on issueD. With no messages to interpret and repeated opponent bids favoring valueC on high-weight issues, I will hold firm on my important issues while varying issueD, where valueA and valueB are equally good for me, to avoid repeating my last exact bid.

I am targeting my maximum utility because it is still early (t=0.20) and the opponent has not shown meaningful concession, repeatedly offering low-value bids for me. I choose issueD: valueB because it is equally good as valueA for me and the opponent has repeatedly included valueB there, while holding firm on my high-importance preferences elsewhere.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid around 0.98 because it is still early at t=0.22 and the opponent has not meaningfully conceded, repeatedly offering very low values for issueA/B/C/F. I make only a small concession on issueA from valueA to valueB while holding firm on the most valuable structure and keeping issueD at valueB, which the opponent has repeatedly included.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.96 because it is still early at t=0.24, but I am making a modest concession on low-weight issueF to avoid stalemate. The opponent has not sent messages and their offers are essentially holding firm, only alternating issueD while consistently giving me poor values on issueA/B/C/F, so I am holding my most important positions while offering D=valueB, which they have repeatedly proposed.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.98 because it is still early-mid negotiation time and the opponent has not shown meaningful concession, repeatedly offering very low values on issueA, issueB, issueC, and issueF. I concede slightly on issueA from my top value to valueB while holding firm on the most valuable structure elsewhere, and keep issueD at valueB since it costs me nothing versus valueA and matches part of the opponent’s pattern.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.964 because it is still early-mid negotiation at t=0.28 and the opponent has not meaningfully conceded, repeatedly offering very low-value packages for me. Their bids suggest they may tolerate issueD=valueB and issueE=valueA, so I keep those while making a small concession on low-weight issueF; there are no messages to weigh.

I am targeting about 0.90 utility: still strong for me at t=0.30, but with meaningful concessions on lower-cost dimensions. The opponent has held firm around very low-utility offers for me, repeatedly asking for valueC on several issues, so I concede issueF to valueC and issueA to valueB while holding my most important/high-utility positions on issueB and issueC.

At t=0.32, I am still targeting a high-utility bid because the opponent has not meaningfully conceded; their offers alternate only on issueD while keeping issueA/B/C/F at my low values. I will make a modest concession on the lower-weight issueF to valueC, which the opponent has repeatedly signaled, while holding firm on my highest-value positions for the more important issues.

I am targeting about 0.90 utility: enough concession for the mid-early stage while still protecting the high-weight issues, especially issueB and issueC. The opponent has not meaningfully conceded and keeps offering very low-value combinations for me, so I am only conceding on lower-cost points: issueD=valueB is equally good for me, issueF=valueC matches their repeated demand despite low utility, and issueA=valueB is a small concession. No opponent messages were provided.

I am targeting a high but slightly more conciliatory utility around 0.89, since we are still before the midpoint but repeated firm offers have not moved the opponent. The opponent’s valid offers consistently push issueA/issueB/issueC/issueF toward valueC while leaving issueE at valueA and sometimes issueD at valueB, so I concede on the less costly issueB alternative and issueF while holding my strongest positions on issueA and issueC.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.90 while still making selective concessions, since time is only at 0.38 and there is no need yet to accept the opponent’s very low-utility pattern. The opponent has not meaningfully conceded: they keep offering valueC on issueA/issueB/issueC/issueF, with only issueD alternating between valueB and valueC, so I will hold firm on the most valuable issues while conceding issueF and using issueD=valueB, which they have repeatedly proposed.

I am targeting a still-high utility around 0.87 while making a moderate mid-game concession because time is at 0.40 and the opponent has not meaningfully improved their offers. Their bids repeatedly demand valueC on most issues, so I am conceding on lower-cost margins like issueF and a small tradeoff on issueB, while holding firm on important high-utility items such as issueC and issueD.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.71 while making a noticeable mid-game concession because time is approaching the halfway point and the opponent has not improved their offers. Their bids consistently demand valueC on issueA/issueB/issueC/issueF and valueA on issueE, so I concede issueF and issueD to valueC while holding firm on issueC and only partially conceding issueA/issueB.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.66: a meaningful concession from my prior offer, but still well above the opponent’s low-value repeated bids. The opponent has not conceded and repeatedly signals valueC on several issues, so I am conceding on issueD and issueF to valueC and moving issueC down to valueB while holding stronger positions on issueA/issueB and keeping issueE at the mutually repeated valueA.

I am targeting a moderate-utility compromise around 0.52 rather than accepting the opponent’s very low-utility offers, since we are only mid-negotiation but the opponent has remained highly rigid. Their repeated bids strongly signal they want valueC on issueA/issueB/issueC/issueF and valueA on issueE, so I concede issueA and keep issueE aligned while still protecting issueB and issueC where concessions are costly.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.68, conceding on issueA and issueF while holding stronger positions on issueB, issueC, and issueD. The opponent has not meaningfully conceded and keeps offering mostly valueC with issueE fixed at valueA, so I should not collapse to their low-utility bid yet at t=0.48, but I will avoid repeating my exact previous offer.

At t=0.50 the opponent has held firm on issueA/B/C/F as valueC and issueE as valueA, with issueD recently consistently valueC, so I am making a mid-game concession on issueD while preserving some value on issueB and issueC. This bid targets moderate utility for me while moving closer to their repeated pattern and avoids repeating my prior exact offer.

At t=0.52 I am targeting a moderate concession bid around 0.684 utility for me, rather than dropping to the opponent’s very low-utility repeated position. The opponent has shown almost no meaningful concession and consistently pushes issueA/B/C/F to valueC while allowing issueE=valueA and sometimes issueD=valueB, so I concede on lower/previously-contested areas but hold issueB and issueC above valueC and use issueD=valueB, which they have offered before.

I am targeting about 0.60 utility: enough above reservation while making a meaningful mid-game concession. The opponent has held almost completely firm on issueA/issueB/issueC/issueF as valueC and issueE as valueA, with only issueD toggling, so I match their latest acceptable-looking issueD=valueB and concede issueC to valueC while holding my most valuable issueB=valueB. There are no messages to weigh, so I rely only on the offer pattern.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.52 rather than fully matching the opponent’s low-utility repeated bid, since we are past the midpoint but not yet at the deadline. The opponent has been essentially holding firm on issueA/issueB/issueC/issueF as valueC and accepting issueE:valueA, with issueD alternating, so I concede on several of their signaled priorities while still protecting issueB and partially protecting issueC.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.60 by conceding on issueA, issueC, issueF, and keeping issueE aligned, while holding issueB at my top value because it is still a high-weight issue for me. At t=0.58 there is meaningful time pressure, but not enough to accept the opponent’s very low repeated offers; their history shows they are holding firm on valueC for most issues, with occasional willingness to use issueD:valueB, which is also excellent for me.

I am targeting a modest positive utility of about 0.44 because we are past the midpoint and the opponent has shown almost no concession, repeatedly insisting on valueC for issues A/B/C/F and valueA for E, with only D alternating between B and C. Given the time pressure, I concede on most of their signaled priorities and match their latest D=valueC, but I hold issueB at valueB because it is a high-weight issue and my best value there.

I am targeting about 0.44 utility, which is a significant concession but still above reservation value at t=0.62. The opponent has held firm on issueA/valueC, issueB/valueC, issueC/valueC, issueE/valueA, and issueF/valueC, while sometimes offering issueD/valueB, so I am conceding the issues they consistently demand and holding only issueD at a value they have already signaled as acceptable.
